title: Generic view-state store keyed (fitting, instanceId) + terminal cwd/scrollback proof

tests.summary: 9 passed — PERSIST_OK roundtrip-1 (simulated restart via vi.resetModules, exact-match read from disk), NO_SAVE_BUTTON_OK (debounced auto-write only, coalesced last-wins), flush-on-shutdown, delete semantics, garbage-shape tolerance, terminal helper cwd+scrollback byte-identical base64 round-trip (PERSIST_OK sess-roundtrip). Full suite 486 passed / 3 skipped.

typecheck.summary: 0 errors (incl. hand-written .d.mts for the fitting helper)

e2e.summary: live boot proof in lieu of browser e2e for this backend slice: real terminal server in sandbox GARRISON_HOME — create session, emit output, SIGTERM (flush), reboot, session rehydrated with SAME instance id 4172ef57-…, name, cwd; scrollback contained the live marker. UI-level e2e lands with W4 which exercises /api/view-state from the browser.

by: N/A — no Garrison-shell UI change this slice
designAudit.notes: usePersistedViewState is a hook; first visual consumer is W4

video.note: W-wave consolidated walkthrough (11 beats, all vision-verified, unflagged). Per-slice beats: W1 instance refs visible in pane headers (beat 9); W2 toggles/no-save/restored session (beats 1-5); W3 eager toggles + server-boot terminal (beats 1-4); W4 workspace switch/add-pane/reload/live-terminal-pane (beats 6-9).

notes: Store: ~/.garrison/view-state/<fittingId>/<instanceId>.json, writeJsonAtomic + readFileTolerant, 500ms trailing debounce (server lib + client hook), flush on shutdown/unmount. Terminal (own-port, standalone package) mirrors the contract via its own view-state.mjs — same pattern as its ui-fittings status file. D1 honoured: PTY dies, fresh shell respawns at restored cwd (live cwd probed via lsof at persist time), scrollback replays with a dim restore marker, recorded command NOT re-run (destructive). State lifecycle: explicit kill/natural exit delete state; detach-reap + shutdown preserve it. Known v1 limit logged: a detach-reaped session disappears from /sessions until the next server boot rehydrates it.
